metric_value: Up to 32 processing modules per Stratus system, connected via StrataLINK high-speed coax
Memory + 2 Motorola 68000 CPUs (software-visible) + ≥1 disk + peripheral controllers; CPU board contains 4 actual 68000 dies (2 self-checking pairs)
notes: Pair-and-spare hardware redundancy at the chip level

metric_value: Fully redundant / partially redundant / non-redundant; configurable per module; reconfiguration on-line without affecting running programs
'Multiple modules are used only to achieve greater system capacity; they never serve as backup for other modules.'
notes: Architectural distinction vs. Tandem-style process-pairs across nodes

metric_value: VOS runs in every module; all modules equal; transparent local networking makes the federation appear as a single virtual computer to programs, programmers and users
All VOS service requests have a uniform interface independent of which module performs the work (e.g., file open is identical regardless of disk location)
notes: Anticipates modern location-transparent distributed file systems

metric_value: Single 125 nsec cycle-time high-speed bus implemented as two parallel buses with independent data and control-logic paths

Each board runs two parallel sets of logic; on output, results are compared; mismatch lights red LED, raises bus interrupt, takes board off-line; redundant partner continues; no other component is aware
Single CPU board = 4 Motorola 68000s organized as 2 software-visible CPUs; redundant virtual/physical address-translation maps; redundant partner CPU board for board-level fault tolerance
Approximately $100 per Motorola 68000 in 1983
notes: Cited by Kastner as the economic enabler: redundancy that 'was not practical until VLSI'

metric_value: Redundant config: N MB program-visible memory implemented as 2N MB physical, split across 2 controllers; 64K RAMs on 2MB boards; 375 nsec read cycle; 4-way interleaved; redundancy can be turned on/off dynamically
Each disk can have a duplicate on a separate controller; writes go to both; reads come from the disk that is not busy or whose heads are best positioned; read errors retried from mirror
notes: Effectively RAID-1 with optimization, predating Patterson/Gibson/Katz 1988 RAID paper

metric_value: Dual StrataLINKs run as parallel separate links; on failure of one, data are retransmitted over the survivor without affecting users

On hard failure, maintenance software process automatically calls the Stratus national service center and transmits a data packet with site and failure info; 'Stratus service people know within a minute of customer failures'
notes: Anticipates modern AIOps / IoT predictive-maintenance call-home telemetry

metric_value: A failed board can be replaced in a running system by a non-technical person without special tools and without affecting any user's program; VOS dynamically reconfigures
notes: Hot-swap modules in 1983 — well ahead of mainstream

metric_value: Application programs may be written in COBOL, PL/I, BASIC, FORTRAN or Pascal; all language features usable, including I/O statements
Multi-tasking, multiple transaction servers, large program address space; terminal-handling requesters and application servers can reside anywhere within a system or network of systems

Single message queue can connect any number of servers with any number of requesters; queues redirect dynamically by changing the pathname to point to another module — applications scale without recompile
notes: Anticipates queue-based microservices and async messaging

metric_value: Phase I writes all updated records to disk and sets 'Phase I Commit' flag in file header; original disk image preserved; all involved nodes must report Phase I success before VOS authorizes Phase II commit; restart-salvage detects Phase I Commit flag
START / COMMIT / ABORT primitives in TPF; ABORT restores all files to pre-START state regardless of whether data is on a single processing module or distributed
System administrator can permit, permit-after-network-password, or deny incoming requests on each system node; access-control lists enforced across the network
Every file has an access-control list of (user-id, rights ∈ {execute, read, read/write}); per-user or per-group; no embedded passwords in programs; enforced regardless of access program/command
notes: ACL approach now standard in POSIX/NTFS

metric_value: 'Stratus Computer uses hardware to detect failures before incorrect data can corrupt processing and databases. Redundant hardware allows the Stratus/32 to continue processing without performance loss in spite of a component failure.'
notes: Kastner's closing summary; the architectural thesis of Stratus

metric_name: Synergy of HW fault-tolerance + system software
metric_value: 'The synergy of hardware-based fault tolerance and high data integrity system software creates an efficient and friendly transaction processing environment.'
notes: Verified prediction: this synergy thesis became canonical in HA/transaction systems; lineage runs through Stratus ftServer, HP NonStop, mainframe-class cloud.

metric_value: Verified: location transparency (paths resolve to remote storage) became universal in NFS, AFS, DFS, modern object stores and global file systems
notes: Verified outcome for OBS-006 framework factor

metric_value: Verified: 2PC is the textbook distributed-commit protocol in 2026; XA standard, modern systems still implement Phase I/Phase II with prepare-flag durability
Verified: automatic call-home telemetry for predictive maintenance is now ubiquitous in enterprise hardware (HPE, Dell EMC, NetApp, IBM, Cisco, hyperscalers)

Verified: disk mirroring (RAID-1) was formalized by Patterson/Gibson/Katz 1988 and became the default redundancy mode for transactional systems through the 2010s; SSD/NVMe and erasure coding now dominate at hyperscale but mirroring remains in transactional tiers
